category_title: Non-ambulant adolescent with Rett Syndrome and profound learning difficulties communicating via eye gaze and vocalizations
minimal_context: Evie is transitioning back to the classroom to join her class after being in the pool.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Evie, we are back from the pool, so it is time to join the class.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (The noises progressively become longer and louder)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We have to join the others now.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Starts to cry, continuing to cry for 19 minutes)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Evie, we are back from the pool, so it is time to join the class.

STUDENT: (Makes sharp, small noises)

TUTOR: Let's go inside.

STUDENT: (The noises progressively become longer and louder)

TUTOR: We have to join the others now.

STUDENT: (Starts to cry, continuing to cry for 19 minutes)

task.content: Joining class after transitioning from the pool

context.environment: Classroom and walking around the school
context.social_setting: Joining her class, interacting with a staff member

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When Evie joined her class after transitioning from the pool
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: She started to make sharp, small noises, but these progressively became longer and louder and then she started to cry. Evie continued crying for 19 minutes.

behavior_patterns.condition: During 'must do' activities that potentially have little room for flexibility, choice and autonomy
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Social emotional growth scores were rated to be the lowest (40%)
